old: Premium support responds within four hours.
new: Premium support responds within twelve hours.
label: conflict
note: spelled-out response-time SLA changed 4 -> 12 hours, no digits

old: New hires complete onboarding in five business days.
new: New hires complete onboarding in ten business days.
label: conflict

old: The starter plan includes two team seats.
new: The starter plan includes five team seats.
label: conflict

old: Referral bonuses pay out fifty dollars per signup.
new: Referral bonuses pay out one hundred dollars per signup.
label: conflict
note: spelled-out bonus amount changed fifty -> one hundred dollars

old: Concierge calls run for twenty minutes each.
new: Concierge calls run for an unlimited duration.
label: conflict
note: fixed spelled-out duration replaced by unlimited, no digit or negation cue

old: Loyalty tier upgrades require six qualifying purchases.
new: Loyalty tier upgrades require nine qualifying purchases.
label: conflict

old: The API allows sixty requests per minute.
new: The API permits up to sixty calls each minute.
label: none
note: same spelled-out rate limit, paraphrased wording

old: Field technicians carry three spare parts kits.
new: Field technicians are equipped with three spare-parts kits per van.
label: none
note: same spelled-out count, added scope detail that does not change the value

old: Standard shipping takes three business days.
new: Standard shipping takes one business week.
label: conflict
note: 3 business days vs 1 business week (5 days) conflicts, unit conversion needed

old: The subscription renews every quarter.
new: The subscription renews every four months.
label: conflict
note: quarter (3 months) conflicts with four months

old: Cold storage items must ship within one day.
new: Cold storage items must ship within twenty-four hours.
label: none
note: one day equals twenty-four hours; compatible unit conversion

old: Passes are valid for a full year.
new: Passes are valid for ten months.
label: conflict
note: a full year (12 months) conflicts with ten months

old: The warranty covers two years of use.
new: The warranty covers eighteen months of use.
label: conflict
note: two years (24 months) conflicts with eighteen months

old: Delivery windows are quoted in half-day blocks.
new: Delivery windows are quoted in twelve-hour blocks.
label: none
note: half a day equals twelve hours; compatible unit conversion

old: Data exports are retained for ninety days.
new: Data exports are retained for two months.
label: conflict
note: ninety days (~3 months) conflicts with a two-month retention window

old: The grace period lasts two weeks.
new: The grace period lasts ten days.
label: conflict
note: two weeks (14 days) conflicts with a ten-day grace period

old: The holiday return window extends through the middle of January.
new: The holiday return window extends through the end of January.
label: conflict
note: return-window deadline moved, no numeric or negation cue

old: Promotional pricing is valid through the end of this quarter.
new: Promotional pricing ended last month.
label: conflict
note: promotion state changed from active to ended

old: The beta program is open to new signups.
new: The beta program closed to new signups last week.
label: conflict

old: Support hours are extended during the holiday season.
new: Support hours returned to normal after the new year.
label: none
note: compatible sequential timeline: extended hours are scoped to the holiday season, and the return to normal is after it ends

old: Store hours changed for the holiday season.
new: Store hours will return to normal after the holidays.
label: none
note: compatible sequential facts describing a planned transition

old: Annual maintenance is scheduled for March each year.
new: Annual maintenance was moved to June starting this year.
label: conflict

old: The legacy API remains supported for existing integrations.
new: The legacy API reached end-of-life last month.
label: conflict

old: The onboarding guide was last revised in the spring.
new: The onboarding guide will be reviewed again in the fall.
label: none
note: compatible timeline facts about past revision and future review

old: Membership dues are billed annually every January.
new: Membership dues are now billed monthly starting this quarter.
label: conflict

old: Refunds are processed by the issuing bank.
new: Refunds are processed by the receiving bank.
label: conflict
note: single-word swap, high lexical overlap hides a real party change

old: Escalations route to the regional manager on duty.
new: Escalations route to the regional manager off duty.
label: conflict
note: on/off flip is not a listed negation cue, easy to miss lexically

old: Cancellations are not subject to a fee within 24 hours.
new: Customers who cancel within a day pay nothing.
label: none
note: different negation-flavored surface forms that actually agree

old: New accounts get a $25 welcome credit.
new: First-time customers start with zero promotional balance.
label: conflict
note: low lexical overlap disguises a direct conflict on the same subject

old: The mobile app supports biometric login.
new: Fingerprint and face unlock are available in the app.
label: none
note: low lexical overlap but a compatible paraphrase, not an unrelated addition

old: Support tickets are resolved by the same agent end-to-end.
new: Support tickets are handed off between agents by specialty.
label: conflict
note: moderate overlap on 'support tickets' hides a conflicting process claim

old: Only verified sellers can list items over $500.
new: Any seller, verified or not, can list items over $500.
label: conflict
note: embedded negation-flavored phrase amid high lexical overlap

old: Sellers must complete verification before listing high-value items.
new: High-value listings require seller verification first.
label: none
note: reordered paraphrase, compatible despite surface restructuring

old: Employees can request PTO with two weeks notice.
new: Employees can request PTO with at least two weeks notice, when possible.
label: none
note: softened qualifier added; compatible with the original policy

old: The API rate limit applies per account.
new: The API rate limit applies per account across all of its API keys.
label: none
note: clarifying refinement of scope, not a narrowing conflict

old: Refund requests are reviewed by the support team.
new: Refund requests over $200 get extra review by the support team.
label: none
note: adds detail for a subset without contradicting the general claim
